Internal Board & Compliance Note — Lotus Meridian Holdings Limited (Main Board code: H-9X23), dated 2026-05-30; subject to Part XIVA SFO controls and Listing Rule 13.09 disclosure procedures; circulation limited to Board, Company Secretary, and Group Legal.
Transaction update: LMH proposes to acquire 18.5% of Brilliant Cove Logistics Technology Co., Ltd. via Northbridge Transfer Partners Ltd.; information herein is specific to a corporation, non-public, and likely to be price-material pending diligence, hence MNPI until announcement; draft timetable targets pre-conditional MOU execution in late June.
HK context: draft inside information assessment is being prepared for the Exchange filing pack; if the price-sensitive threshold is met, we will pivot to a holding announcement with a confidentiality wall for the data room and a clean team protocol consistent with the Takeovers Code.
Quasi-identifier cluster (PDPO risk): Mr. Quillon Pak‑Lam Sze, Associate Director, Corporate Development (LMH-EMP-0 0 9 4), office: Quarry Bay, HK; internal OMS acct “QSZ_90”; email quillon.sze@lotus-meridian.hk; partial DOB 1990‑09; device label “LMH‑MBP‑14‑QSZ‑229c”; last login via vpn sess ref hks-0-1903 from Kowloon East.
Additional contextual signals re Mr. Sze: intranet URL hit https://intra.lmh.local/sess?user=QSZ_90&tag=H K E X - 1 3 - 0 9 on 2026‑05‑28 19:41 HKT; Teams room “Blue Swallow – M&A”; calendar slot “LMH x Brilliant Cove diligence (room 21F‑C)”; please avoid combining these in external emails.
Separated weak identifiers (do not attribute to an individual): device types noted in the clean room include “iPhone 12 mini” and “ThinkPad X13”; generic city reference “Central”; sample IP 203.86.1xx.45; month of birth “March”; none of these alone should be treated as identifying data.
PDPO handling: redact DOBs and personal emails from the VDR indices; where necessary, use role accounts (e.g., corpdev-role@lotus-meridian.hk) and device pool tags; confirm the personal data collection statement in Annex A applies to cross-border transfers to the diligence vendor.
Fund workflow: Harbor Pine Fund SPC – Sub‑Fund A (internal code HPF-A‑001122) is invited to co-invest via an SPV subject to a side letter; no client bank details or national IDs are included; KYC will be run through a separate secure channel after term sheet stage.
Employment overlay: draft conditional retention for Ms. Seraphine Ko‑Yi Tam, CFO designate of Brilliant Cove, includes a 24‑month retention cash award in HK$ bands and standard non-solicit; no DOB recorded; contact for HR queries: hr‑tam@brilliantcove.com.hk.
Public-only materials: the marketing pack for the potential co-investors contains summary charts from publicly available freight indices and does not include MNPI; any model outputs relying on internal forecast spreads will be ring‑fenced.
Obfuscation and access strings: brill‑cove‑data‑room\2026‑0 6‑APAC; link token lmh%2Dqs z%2F1990-09 is system‑generated and should not be emailed externally; convene via bridge code “LMH‑M&A‑7831” only.
Note re device data: the placeholder MAC “00:00:00:00:00:00” in the IT appendix is illustrative and not a real MAC; no live MAC addresses will be logged in the external clean room per IT Security’s instruction.
Governance: any pre‑announcement sounding with investors will require wall‑cross consent; Company Secretary to maintain the insider list; Legal to clear press Q&As; no press approaches are authorised at this stage.
For queries on MNPI controls contact compliance@lotus-meridian.hk or the undersigned on +852 3 6 8 9 9 2 7 1; for data room access, route through deal‑ops@northbridge‑tp.com only.
